mnemonics.title: STAKEHOLDER Categories
mnemonics.content: I P E S S: Internal (team, managers), Primary (sponsor, user), External (customer, supplier), Secondary (community, competitors), Stakeholder interests vary

acronyms.expansion: Project Management Body of Knowledge - defines stakeholder management as identifying people/groups that can impact or are impacted by the project
acronyms.usage: Reference for standardized stakeholder management practices

acronyms.expansion: Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed - matrix used to define roles and responsibilities of stakeholders
acronyms.usage: Clarifying stakeholder responsibilities in project teams

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a 2x2 table: Top-left = High Power/High Interest (manage closely), Top-right = High Power/Low Interest (keep satisfied), Bottom-left = Low Power/High Interest (keep informed), Bottom-right = Low Power/Low Interest (monitor)

Think of concentric circles: Inner circle = Primary stakeholders (directly involved), Middle circle = Secondary stakeholders (indirectly affected), Outer circle = External stakeholders (outside organization)
A circular flow: Identify → Analyze → Plan → Engage → Monitor → Repeat

analogies.analogy: Like making a guest list for a party - you need to think about everyone who might want to come or might be affected by the event
Like managing different relationships: Close friends (high interest, high power), VIP clients (high power, low interest), Curious neighbors (low power, high interest), General public (neither)
Like a relationship - requires regular communication, understanding needs, and maintaining trust throughout the journey
